question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/ContractResultRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: save() (CrudRepository)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a contract call with a crafted transactionHash of all-zero bytes in ContractResult, causing Hashgraph history to record a null-equivalent hash that collides with other zero-hash records and corrupts the uniqueness invariant of the contract_transaction_hash table?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/ContractResultRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: prune(long consensusTimestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a time-of-check/time-of-use race between the RetentionJob's findLatestWithOffset() call and the subsequent prune() execution to delete ContractResult records that were just inserted, creating gaps in Hashgraph history that appear as missing transactions?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/ContractResultRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: prune(long consensusTimestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user cause the importer to process a record-stream file with a consensusEnd timestamp far in the past, causing prune() to be called with a very small consensusTimestamp that deletes no records but advances the prune cursor, preventing future legitimate pruning from reclaiming space and causing storage exhaustion?
